note:
惠普企业有限公司是最新的科技公司，将重点从硅谷转移到了硅谷，该公司周二宣布将其总部从加利福尼亚州圣何塞迁至德克萨斯州休斯敦。
 休斯敦（HPE）是美国最大的就业中心，是一个吸引和留住未来多样化人才的诱人市场，也是公司目前正在建设最先进的新园区的好地方。该公司在其第四季度财报中表示。目前尚不清楚此举将影响多少员工，尽管该公司表示此举不会裁员。
  HPE表示，HPE将保留圣何塞校区，并将合并那里的某些湾区基地。
   该公司还提高了2021财年的指导。盘后交易中股价几乎没有变化。
 冠状病毒大流行给许多科技公司和著名的硅谷人物提供了退出加利福尼亚的借口。没有很多人每天都需要去办公室，许多人质疑高昂的生活成本以及随着向偏远地区工作的广泛转移而产生的国家高额税收。
 数据分析软件公司Palantir Technologies今年早些时候将其总部从科罗拉多州的丹佛市从加利福尼亚州的帕洛阿尔托市迁出。该公司的联合创始人乔·朗斯代尔（Joe Lonsdale）也紧随其后，并于上个月宣布将风险投资公司8VC的总部从旧金山迁至德克萨斯州奥斯汀。
  据报道，Dropbox首席执行官德鲁·休斯顿（Drew Houston）也决定搬到奥斯汀。 Dropbox在10月表示将停止要求员工进入其办公室，而是将远程工作作为标准做法。对于需要亲自见面或一起工作的员工，公司将设立＆＃34; Dropbox Studios＆＃34;可以安全地在旧金山，西雅图，奥斯丁和都柏林进行操作。 
一些公司还允许员工在工作场所拥有更大的灵活性，同时保留办公空间。  Twitter和Square使员工永远在家工作， 微软表示，员工将拥有更多的在家办公的灵活性。 Facebook首席执行官马克·扎克伯格（Mark Zuckerberg）于5月预测，未来十年内50％的员工将在远程工作。
note_en:
Hewlett Packard Enterprise is the latest tech company to shift its focus away from Silicon Valley, announcing Tuesday that it will relocate its headquarters from San Jose, California, to Houston, Texas.
 "HPE's largest U.S. employment hub, Houston is an attractive market to recruit and retain future diverse talent, and is where the company is currently constructing a state-of-the-art new campus," the company said in its fourth quarter earnings release. It's unclear how many employees the move will affect, though the company said no layoffs will be with the move.
  HPE will keep the San Jose campus, and will consolidate some of its Bay Area sites there, it said.
   The company also raised guidance for the 2021 fiscal year. Shares were little changed in after hours trading.
 The coronavirus pandemic has given a number of tech companies and prominent Silicon Valley figures an excuse to exit California. Without many needing to go into an office every day, many are questioning the high cost of living and the state's hefty taxes amid a broader shift to remote work.
 Data analytics software company  Palantir Technologies  moved its headquarters from Denver, Colorado from Palo Alto, California, earlier this year. The company's co-founder  Joe Lonsdale followed suit, and announced last month he was  moving the headquarters of 8VC, his venture firm, from San Francisco to Austin, Texas.
  Dropbox CEO Drew Houston has also  reportedly decided to move to Austin.  Dropbox said in October it will  stop asking employees to come into its offices and instead make remote work the standard practice. For employees who need to meet or work together in person, the company is setting up "Dropbox Studios" in San Francisco, Seattle, Austin and Dublin when it's safe to do so.
 Some companies are also allowing employees more flexibility with where they work, while retaining office space.
 Twitter and  Square are letting employees work from home "forever," while  Microsoft said workers will have  more flexibility to work from home.  Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg predicted in May that 50% of employees will be working remotely within the next decade.
title: HPE说它将总部从圣何塞迁至休斯敦
title_en: HPE Says It's Relocating HQ To Houston From San Jose

note:
在当局偶然发现一个自由​​站立在犹他州沙漠中的神秘金属物体不到两周后-几天后，它消失了-据报道，类似的巨石在世界范围的一半处。罗马尼亚城市皮亚特拉·尼姆特（Piatra Neamt）的居民说，他们发现了另一本可能从2001年起就被偷走过的奇怪物品：太空漫游。
  当地报纸报道说，这块巨石像在犹他州发现的那块巨石一样，高约10到12英尺，显然完全由微弱的反射金属组成。据报道，它被发现站在BâtcaDoamnei高原上，靠近俯瞰该城市的考古遗址。当地广播电台Jurnal FM记录了所声称物体的视频，在其表面上散发着令人眼前一亮的光泽和环形标记。
    ＆＃34;没有理由为那些认为宇宙中仍然存在生命的人感到恐慌，＆＃34;根据《独立报》的翻译，Carabelea打趣了。这项发现在周五浮出水面后被发布到了Facebook。
  ＆＃34;我的猜测是，一些外星人，厚脸皮和可怕的少年与父母一起离开了家。 UFO并开始在全球范围内种植金属巨石。首先在犹他州，然后在Piatra Neamt，＆＃34;他加了。 ＆＃34;我很荣幸他们选择了我们的城市。＆＃34;
       州公共安全部的官员首次发现犹他州的巨石后不到10天就消失了。当局在犹他州的红岩之乡表示了自己的完全困惑。
  星期五晚上突然消失，他们同样感到困惑。据《纽约时报》报道，一名冒险摄影师说，他发现有四名男子正在拆除该结构。
  周六早上遗留在建筑物中的全部是＆＃34;曾经在顶部的三角形金属片，以及塔底所在的孔，＆＃34;根据盐湖论坛报。土地管理局拒绝移动该结构。 
至于罗马尼亚的巨石，卡拉贝拉说，尽管他自己没有任何答案，但如果新发现的谜团吸引游客来到他的城市，他也不会感到沮丧。  ＆＃34;除了可能诱人的阴谋论之外，＆＃34; 他补充说，我希望看到这种随机性，进一步证明我们的城市很特别。 对于地球人和（也许）不仅如此。
note_en:
Less than two weeks after authorities  stumbled across a mysterious metal object standing freely in the Utah desert — and just days after  it disappeared — a similar monolith has been reported nearly halfway around the world. Residents in the Romanian city of Piatra Neamt say they have found another odd item that could have been ripped from the set of  2001: A Space Odyssey .
  The  local newspaper reports that the monolith, like the one discovered in Utah, is about 10 to 12 feet tall and apparently composed entirely of a dimly reflective metal. It was reportedly found standing on the Bâtca Doamnei plateau, near an archaeological site overlooking the city. Jurnal FM, a local radio station,  recorded video of the purported object, revealing an eye-aching sheen and looped markings along its surface.
    "There is no reason to panic for those who think there is still life in the universe," Carabelea quipped, according to a  translation by The Independent. The statement was  posted to Facebook after the discovery surfaced Friday.
  "My guess is that some alien, cheeky and terrible teenagers left home with their parents' UFO and started planting metal monoliths around the world. First in Utah and then at Piatra Neamt," he added. "I am honored that they chose our city."
       The monolith in Utah disappeared less than 10 days after officers from the state's Department of Public Safety first spotted it. Authorities  professed their utter confusion at its presence in Utah's Red Rock Country.
  They were no less confused by its  sudden disappearance overnight Friday. An adventure photographer said that he spotted four men dismantling the structure, according  to The New York Times.
  All that was left of the structure Saturday morning was "a triangular metal piece that used to be on top and a hole where the base of the tower stood," according to  The Salt Lake Tribune. The Bureau of Land Management denied moving the structure.
  As for the monolith in Romania, Carabelea said that while he had no answers himself, he would not be upset if the newfound mystery happened to attract tourists to his city.
  "Beyond the conspiracy theories that can be tempting," he added, "I'd like to see this randomness as further proof that our city is special. For the earthlings and (maybe) not only."
title: 罗马尼亚出现第二个神秘巨石
title_en: A Second, Mysterious Monolith Appears In Romania

note:
马丁·特里普（Martin Tripp）是前特斯拉工人，与首席执行官埃隆·马斯克（Elon Musk）陷入了激烈的法律斗争两年多，在承认向记者泄露机密信息后，被勒令向其前雇主支付40万美元。
 该和解旨在结束特斯拉的一个更肮脏的故事，特里普曾是一名工艺技术员，曾因特斯拉浪费了“下颚”的原材料而被指控与亿万富翁首席执行官locked之以鼻。它提高了Model 3轿车的产量。
 该和解旨在结束特斯拉更多肮脏的故事之一
 马斯克后来指责特里普“破坏”，并亲自下令调查人员入侵特里普的电话并监视他的信息。特斯拉甚至误导了当地警察，称特里普有可能在该公司的内华达州工厂进行大规模枪击。
 但最后，特里普（Tripp）输了。这笔款项是特斯拉在2018年提起的一项诉讼的拟议和解的一部分，该诉讼称特里普入侵了电动汽车公司的系统并将``千兆字节''的数据转移给了第三方。作为协议的一部分，特里普告诉《商业内幕》记者特斯拉在生产Model 3时浪费了大量原材料，他承认违反了与商业秘密和计算机犯罪有关的法律。
 尽管被法官命令停止，特里普还同意向特斯拉支付25,000美元，以继续披露有关该公司的信息。 Tripp一直在线发布大量文档和视频，其中包括许多本案的保密命令。八月份，特里普解雇了他的律师，并着手代理此案。消息还显示，特斯拉的卖空者Funicular基金为Tripp的法律辩护提供资金。
 今年早些时候，一名法官驳回了特里普针对特斯拉的诽谤案，其中前技术人员指责该公司散布关于他的虚假谣言。在特里普向证券交易委员会申请举报人身份后，马斯克通过电子邮件向《卫报》的一名记者发了电子邮件，告诉他们引爆者已与特斯拉联系，称特里普可能在内华达州的超级工厂“回头开枪”。当地警长认为威胁并不真实，但特斯拉发布了新闻稿，并被多家媒体所接受。
note_en:
Martin Tripp, the former Tesla worker who has been embroiled in a bitter legal battle with CEO Elon Musk for over two years, was ordered to pay his former employer $400,000 after admitting to leaking confidential information to a reporter.
 The settlement is intended to bring an end to  one of the more sordid stories at Tesla, in which Tripp, a former process technician, locked horns with the billionaire CEO over allegations that Tesla was wasting a “jaw-dropping” amount of raw material as it ramped up production of the Model 3 sedan.
 The settlement is intended to bring an end to one  ﻿of the more sordid stories at Tesla
 Musk later accused Tripp of “sabotage” and personally ordered investigators to hack Tripp’s phone and spy on his messages. Tesla even misled local police about a potential mass shooting by Tripp at the company’s Nevada factory.
 But in the end, Tripp came out on the losing side. The payment is part of a proposed settlement to  a lawsuit filed by Tesla in 2018 alleging that Tripp hacked the electric car company’s system and transferred “gigabytes” of data to third parties. As part of the agreement, Tripp admitted to violating laws related to trade secrets and computer crimes when he  told a  Business Insider reporter that Tesla was wasting a significant amount of raw materials during production of its Model 3.
 Tripp also agreed to pay $25,000 to Tesla for continuing to reveal information about the company, despite being ordered to stop by a judge. Tripp had been publishing a large number of documents and videos online, including many under a confidentiality order in the case. In August, Tripp fired his lawyers and set about representing himself in the case. It was also revealed that a Tesla short seller,  The Funicular Fund, was financing Tripp’s legal defense.
 Earlier this year,  a judge dismissed Tripp’s defamation case against Tesla, in which the former technician accused the company of spreading false rumors about him. After Tripp filed for whistleblower status with the Securities and Exchange Commission, Musk emailed a reporter at   The Guardian telling them a tipster had contacted Tesla to say that Tripp might “come back and shoot people,” at the Nevada Gigafactory. The local sheriff determined the threat was not real, but Tesla issued a press release, which was picked up by several media outlets.
title: 特斯拉告密者马丁·特里普（Martin Tripp）被判赔偿40万美元
title_en: Tesla Whistleblower Martin Tripp Ordered To Pay $400,000 To Settle Hacking Case

note:
在将Linux引入PlayStation 4之后，著名的开发商Hector Martin（也称为marcan）正在为开源世界的另一个重要项目做准备。
 他希望在Apple Silicon上使用Linux。为了实现这一目标，马丁解释说，他需要一些财务工作，因为这不是一个可以在一夜之间发生的项目。
 “由于这些设备是全新的定制硅片，因此移植Linux以在其上运行是一项艰巨的任务。开发人员解释说，这不仅是一项业余项目，而且是一项全职工作。
 因此，本次募捐活动的整个思路是为开发人员提供必要的资金，以将Linux引入Apple Silicon，从而将项目转变为他的全职工作。
  第一个目标是使Linux在运行M1的Mac mini上运行，但最终，应该支持在Apple Silicon上运行的所有设备。
 “目标是使Apple Silicon Mac上的Linux支持不仅达到技术演示的目的，而且实际上是您希望在日常驱动程序设备上使用的OS。为此，有大量工作要做。在事物上运行Linux很容易，但要使其运行良好则很难。需要为所有设备编写驱动程序。完全自定义的Apple GPU的驱动程序是最复杂的组件，要拥有良好的桌面体验，这是必需的。为了使您的电池寿命合理，电源管理也需要工作良好。”开发人员解释说。
 在您问之前，是的，整个事情都是合法的，并且不需要越狱，因为开发人员只会转向反向工程以使整个事情正常工作。 
根据捐赠的金额，开发人员表示，他想购买新的Apple Silicon驱动的设备，并最终雇用其他人来帮助完成这项工作。
note_en:
After bringing Linux to the PlayStation 4, famous developer Hector Martin, also known as marcan, is getting ready for another important project for the open-source world.
 He wants Linux on Apple Silicon. And to make it happen, Martin explains he’ll need some financial job, as this isn’t a project that can just happen overnight.
 “Since these devices are brand new and bespoke silicon, porting Linux to run on them is a huge undertaking. Well beyond a hobby project, it is a full-time job,” the developer  explains.
 So the whole idea of this fundraising campaign is to provide the developer with the necessary funds to bring Linux to Apple Silicon, essentially turning the project into his full-time job.
  The first target is getting Linux to run on the M1-powered Mac mini, but eventually, all devices running on Apple Silicon are supposed to be supported.
 “The goal is to bring Linux support on Apple Silicon macs to the point where it is not merely a tech demo, but is actually an OS you would want to use on a daily driver device. To do this, there is a huge amount of work to be done. Running Linux on things is easy, but making it work well is hard. Drivers need to be written for all devices. The driver for the completely custom Apple GPU is the most complicated component, which is necessary to have a good desktop experience. Power management needs to work well too, for your battery life to be reasonable,” the dev explains.
 Before you ask, yes, the whole thing is legal and a jailbreak won’t be required, as the developer would only turn to reverse engineering to get the whole thing to work.
 Depending on how much money is donated, the developer says he wants to purchase the new Apple Silicon-powered devices and eventually hire other people to help with the job.
title: 赫克托·马丁（Hector Martin）承诺将Linux引入M1芯片
title_en: Hector Martin Promises To Bring Linux To the M1 Chip

note:
改天，改年终名单。在Google Play商店和Spotify在各自的平台上共享了最重要的内容之后，苹果公司通过其在线商店下载了精选的美国最受欢迎的播客，有声读物和书籍。
 该公司将Code Switch选为年度最佳音频节目。 2020年是苹果第一次以这种方式识别单个播客。 Code Switch是NPR关于种族的每周讨论。尽管该系列节目已经播出了七年的大部分时间，但由于整个美国人民抗议乔治·弗洛伊德（George Floyd）的死和其他种族不公正事件，该系列在整个夏天变得越来越受欢迎。
  与过去几年一样，该公司还分享了人们通过Apple Podcasts听过的最受欢迎的音频节目。像《你应该知道的东西》，《美国生活》和《每日报》这样的老大热门在美国最受欢迎的节目中脱颖而出。当涉及到2020年的新节目时，《解锁我们》，《漂亮的白人父母》和《 CounterClock》在该年度中排名前三。苹果的编辑团队也有发言权。他们选择了《华盛顿邮报》的《加州之恋》，《金丝雀》和《为性而死》作为2020年的最爱。如果您正在寻找新的东西，这三个列表都是一个不错的起点。
 在书籍方面，Brit Bennett的《 The Vanishing Half》，Elin Hilderbrand的《 28 Summers》和Liz Moore的《 Long Bright River》构成了公司的前三名。同时，杰西卡·辛普森（Jessica Simpson）的《打开书》（Open Book），娜塔莎（Natasha Trethewey）的《纪念驱动器》（Memorial Drive）和丹·希思（Dan Heath）的《上游》（Upstream）在有声读物类别中获得了最高荣誉。您可以通过Apple Books和Apple Podcasts查看所有完整列表。
note_en:
Another day, another year-end list. After the  Google Play Store and  Spotify shared the top content on their respective platforms, Apple has chimed in with a selection of the most popular podcasts, audiobooks and books people in the US downloaded through its online stores.
 The company picked    Code Switch  as the best audio show of the year. 2020 is the first time Apple has recognized a  single podcast in this way.  Code Switch is NPR’s weekly discussion on race. While the series has been on the air for the better part of seven years, it became significantly more popular over the summer as people across the US took to protest the death of George Floyd and other instances of racial injustice.
  As in past years, the company also shared a selection of the most popular audio shows people listened to through Apple Podcasts. Few surprises here as old favorites like   Stuff You Should Know ,   This American Life  and   The Daily  came out as the most popular shows in the US. When it comes to  shows new to 2020,   Unlocking Us,   Nice White Parents and   CounterClock made the top three for the year. Apple’s editorial team had their  say as well. They picked   California Love,  Canary by the  Washington Post and   Dying for Sex as their favorites of 2020. If you’re looking for something new to listen to, all three lists are a good place to start.
 When it comes to  books,  The Vanishing Half by Brit Bennett,  28 Summers by Elin Hilderbrand and  Long Bright River by Liz Moore made up the company’s top three. Meanwhile,  Open Book by Jessica Simpson,  Memorial Drive by Natasha Trethewey and  Upstream by Dan Heath took top honors in the audiobooks category. You can see all the full lists through Apple Books and Apple Podcasts.
title: NPR的“代码切换”是苹果年度播客
title_en: 'Code Switch' From NPR Is Apple's Podcast of the Year

note:
Reddit透露它现在每天有5200万用户，并且这个数字似乎正在迅速增长。 Reddit告诉《华尔街日报》，10月份的每日使用量同比增长了44％，这一指标的大幅增长表明该产品对其用户的重要性。
 不过，与其他社交媒体竞争对手相比，这个数字很小。 Twitter每天有1.87亿用户，Snap有2.49亿，Facebook有18.2亿。但是，在规模更大的情况下，这些服务中没有一个能看到每日使用量像Reddit一样快。在最近的几个季度中，Twitter报告同比增长29％，Snap报告18％，Facebook报告12％。
 该公司告诉《华尔街日报》，这是首次首次共享Reddit的每日使用情况，“这是对我们用户增长的更准确反映，并且与行业报告更加一致”。 （的确，社交媒体公司倾向于将每日用户作为首选指标；尽管例如，Twitter只是放弃报告每月用户，因为该数字正在下降，而每日用户却在增长。）发生这一变化的另一个原因专注于可以更好地帮助Reddit发展广告业务的业务。
 Reddit过去专注于每月使用。去年的这个时候，该公司表示每月有4.3亿用户，同比增长30％。在Reddit开始快速迭代其服务的时期内，出现了快速增长。它在2018年进行了重大的重新设计，今年又增加了画廊和民意调查等功能。该公司还更加认真地打击了其网站上托管的一些有问题的社区，这应该有助于使该服务对广告商和用户整体而言更加友好。
note_en:
Reddit revealed that it now has 52 million daily users, and the number appears to be growing quickly. Reddit  told  The Wall Street Journal  that daily usage grew 44 percent year over year for October, a sizable increase on a metric that speaks to just how essential a product it is to its users.
 The number is small compared to other social media rivals, though. Twitter has 187 million daily users, Snap has 249 million, and Facebook has 1.82 billion. But at their larger sizes, none of those services are seeing daily usage grow as rapidly as Reddit. In their most recent quarters, Twitter reported 29 percent year-over-year growth, Snap reported 18 percent, and Facebook reported 12 percent.
 Daily usage of Reddit is being shared for the first time “as a more accurate reflection of our user growth and to be more in-line with industry reporting,” the company told the  Journal. (It is true that social media companies tend to use daily users as their preferred metric; though Twitter, for instance, only switched away from reporting monthly users because that number was dipping, while daily users was growing.) The other reason for the change was to focus on a number that would better help to grow Reddit’s advertising business.
 Reddit has focused on monthly usage in the past. This time last year, the company said it had 430 million monthly users, with 30 percent year-over-year growth. The quick growth has come during a period that Reddit began to iterate more rapidly on its service. It rolled out  a significant redesign in 2018 and this year has added features like  galleries and  polls. The company has also gotten more serious about cracking down on some of the  problematic  communities hosted on its site, which should help to make the service friendlier to both advertisers and users overall.
title: Reddit首次显示每日活跃用户数：5200万
title_en: Reddit Reveals Daily Active User Count For the First Time: 52 Million

note:
华盛顿（路透社） - 福特汽车公司正在敦促主要汽车制造商考虑的出价以吸引业内人士的共识总统当选人拜登上任之前备份对车辆排放的框架协议与加利福尼亚州，根据周一路透社看到的信。
  大型汽车制造商将在周二举行的汽车行业协会虚拟会议上讨论下一步行动，这是在通用汽车公司突然宣布将不再支持特朗普政府为禁止加利福尼亚州制定自己的汽车排放法规而进行的努力之后一周。
 2019年10月，通用汽车加入了丰田汽车公司，菲亚特克莱斯勒汽车公司和其他汽车制造商的行列，以支持美国总统唐纳德·特朗普在加利福尼亚的斗争。
 福特，本田汽车公司，大众汽车公司和宝马于2019年7月与加利福尼亚州达成了一项自愿协议，同意在2026车型年内减少车辆排放，这将使它们达到单一的全国标准。这些目标将低于奥巴马时代制定的目标，但高于特朗普政府的回滚目标。
  福特美洲区总裁库马尔·加尔霍特拉（Kumar Galhotra）在此前未报的一封信中说，拜登（Biden）获胜后，关于特朗普努力抢占加利福尼亚汽车排放量的努力“至少在接下来的几年里，现在基本上是没有定论的。因此，更相关的问题是标准的问题。”
  他补充说：“拜登政府不会放任特朗普标准，并且通过诉讼和/或监管重启，新团队将朝着一个更严格的方向发展。”
  福特发言人拒绝对此书发表评论，但表示，加州协议“应成为新法规的基础，因为拜登政府认为2021年的燃油经济性标准将提高。” 
加州空气资源委员会主席玛丽·尼科尔斯（Mary Nichols）本月告诉路透社，该州与汽车制造商的排放协议可以作为联邦标准的“好模板”。  环境与公共工程委员会高级民主党参议员汤姆·卡珀（Tom Carper）表示：“其他汽车制造商应结束对州政府的挑战，并接受加利福尼亚框架，以此作为制定标准的第一步，使我们达到需要的目标 。”  通用，菲亚特·克莱斯勒和丰田拒绝对福特的来信发表评论。 丰田汽车在上周的诉讼中表示，“鉴于情况的变化，我们正在评估情况。”
note_en:
WASHINGTON (Reuters) - Ford Motor Co is urging major automakers to consider backing a framework deal with California on vehicle emissions in a bid to reach industry consensus before President-elect Joe Biden takes office, according to a letter seen by Reuters on Monday.
  Major automakers are set to discuss next steps at a virtual meeting of their auto trade association Tuesday, which comes a week after General Motors Co abruptly announced it would no longer back the Trump administration’s ongoing effort to bar California from setting its own vehicle emissions rules.
 In October 2019, GM joined Toyota Motor Corp, Fiat Chrysler Automobiles NV and other automakers in backing President Donald Trump in the California fight.
 Ford, Honda Motor Co, Volkswagen AG and BMW in July 2019 struck a voluntary agreement with California on reducing vehicle emissions through the 2026 model years that would allow them to meet a single nationwide standard. The targets would be lower than Obama-era rules through but higher than the Trump administration’s rollback.
  In a previously unreported letter, Ford Americas President Kumar Galhotra on Wednesday said with Biden’s win, the fight over Trump’s effort to preempt California on vehicle emissions “is now, at least for the next set of years, essentially moot. The more relevant issue is thus the question of the standards.”
  He added: “The Biden Administration will not let the Trump standards stand, and either by way of litigation and/or a regulatory reboot, the new team will move in a different, more stringent direction.”
  A Ford spokeswoman declined comment on the letter but said the California agreement “should be the foundation for new regulations as the Biden administration considers stronger fuel economy standards in 2021.”
 California Air Resources Board Chair Mary Nichols told Reuters this month the state’s emissions agreement with automakers could serve as a “good template” for federal standards.
 Senator Tom Carper, the top Democrat on the Environment and Public Works Committee, said “the rest of the automakers should end their challenges to state authority and embrace the California framework as the first step in setting standards that get us to where we need to be.”
 GM, Fiat Chrysler and Toyota declined comment on Ford’s letter. Toyota said last week of the lawsuit that “given the changing circumstances, we are assessing the situation.”
title: 福特呼吁汽车制造商支持加州燃油经济协议
title_en: Ford Calls On Automakers To Support California Fuel Economy Deal

由澳大利亚科学家开发的功能强大的新型望远镜以创纪录的速度绘制了300万个星系，揭示了宇宙最深层的秘密。
 澳大利亚平方公里阵列探路者（Askap）首次进行了整个南部天空的调查，打破了记录，在300小时内绘制了大约300万个星系。
 科学家在西澳大利亚内陆的一个观测站用望远镜观察了83％的天空。
  根据望远镜的开发商和运营商澳大利亚科学机构CSIRO的说法，结果是宇宙的新地图集。
 这项快速的Askap连续谱调查已经绘制了数百万个恒星状点。 CSIRO说，大多数是遥远的星系。这些遥远的星系中大约有一百万从未见过。
 CSIRO首席执行官拉里·马歇尔（Larry Marshall）表示，这项调查揭示了宇宙中最深层的秘密。
 马歇尔在一份声明中说：“阿斯卡普（Askap）正在将最新的科学技术应用于有关宇宙奥秘的古老问题，并为世界各地的天文学家提供新的突破来解决挑战。” 
主要作者，CSIRO天文学家戴维·麦康奈尔（David McConnell）表示，科学家期望在未来的调查中发现数以千万计的新星系。
 望远镜以前所未有的速度和细节绘制了天空。 CSIRO说，结果证明，全天候的调查可以在几周内完成，而不是几年。
 该仪器具有特别宽阔的视野，可以拍摄高细节的天空全景照片。望远镜接收器的质量意味着该团队仅需要合并903张图像即可形成完整的天空图。
 其他主要的世界望远镜也需要成千上万张图像才能进行全天候的勘测。
 然后，CSIRO的定制硬件和软件处理了望远镜产生的13.5艾字节（135亿千兆字节）的原始数据。
  马歇尔说，原始数据的生成速度比澳大利亚整个互联网的传输速度快。
 天文学家将能够像社会科学家使用来自国家普查的信息一样，对大量的星系进行统计分析。 
联邦科学技术部长卡伦·安德鲁斯（Karen Andrews）表示，Askap是澳大利亚世界领先的射电天文能力的典范。  她说：“这项新的调查证明，我们已准备好在射电天文学领域取得巨大的飞跃。”  初步结果发表在周二的澳大利亚天文学会刊物上。  澳大利亚人可以在CSIRO网站上进行虚拟的地图游览。
note_en:
A powerful new telescope developed by Australian scientists has mapped three million galaxies in record speed, unlocking the universe’s deepest secrets.
 The Australian Square Kilometre Array Pathfinder (Askap) broke records as it conducted its first survey of the entire southern sky, mapping approximately three million galaxies in 300 hours.
 Scientists used the telescope at an observatory in outback  Western Australia to observe 83% of the sky.
  The result is a new atlas of the universe, according to the telescope’s developer and operator, Australian science agency the  CSIRO.
 The survey – the Rapid Askap Continuum Survey – has mapped millions of star-like points; most are distant galaxies, the CSIRO says. About a million of those distant galaxies have never been seen before.
 The CSIRO’s chief executive, Larry Marshall, said the survey had unlocked the deepest secrets of the universe.
 “Askap is applying the very latest in science and technology to age-old questions about the mysteries of the universe and equipping astronomers around the world with new breakthroughs to solve their challenges,” Marshall said in a statement.
 Scientists expect to find tens of millions of new galaxies in future surveys, lead author and CSIRO astronomer David McConnell said.
 The telescope mapped the sky in unprecedented speed and detail. The CSIRO says the result proves that an all-sky survey can be done in weeks rather than years.
 The instrument has a particularly wide field of view, enabling it to take panoramic pictures of the sky in high detail. The quality of the telescope’s receivers means the team only needed to combine 903 images to form a full map of the sky.
 Other major world telescopes have required tens of thousands of images to put together an all-sky survey.
 The CSIRO’s custom-built hardware and software then processed the 13.5 exabytes (13.5bn gigabytes) of raw data generated by the telescope.
  That raw data was generated at a faster rate than Australia’s entire internet traffic, Marshall said.
 Astronomers will be able to statistically analyse large populations of galaxies the same way social scientists use information from a national census.
 The federal science and technology minister, Karen Andrews, said Askap was an example of Australia’s world-leading radio astronomy capability.
 “This new survey proves that we are ready to make a giant leap forward in the field of radio astronomy,” she said.
 The initial results were published on Tuesday in the journal Publications of the Astronomical Society of Australia.
 Australians can take their own virtual tour of the map on the CSIRO’s website.
title: 澳大利亚望远镜以创纪录的速度绘制了宇宙新地图集
title_en: Australian Telescope Maps New Atlas of the Universe In Record Speed

意大利监管机构已对苹果公司处以1000万欧元（合1200万美元）的罚款，原因是苹果对iPhone的防水性能提出了误导性和不公平的主张。
 意大利反托拉斯当局发现苹果犯有两件事，其中一件比另一件更为严重……
  罚款是由L'AutoritàGarante della Concorrenza e del Mercato（AGCM）处以的，该字面意思是竞争和市场的担保机构。这是竞争监管机构，负责确保公司公平对待消费者和竞争对手。
 首先，它说，苹果公司提出了防水要求，但没有向消费者表明这些要求仅在理想的实验室条件下才是正确的，并且手机在真实条件下没有通过相同的测试。
 第一个问题涉及许多不同型号的iPhone的营销– iPhone 8，iPhone 8 Plus，iPhone XR，iPhone XS，iPhone XS Max，iPhone 11，iPhone 11pro和iPhone 11 pro Max –据称，每个所宣传的产品根据型号的不同，其防水深度最大为4米至1米。长达30分钟。
 但是，据管理局称，这些消息并没有澄清这些主张仅在存在特定条件的情况下才是正确的，例如在使用静态和纯净水的特定和受控实验室测试期间，而不是在正常使用设备的情况下受到消费者的欢迎。
 其次，更重要的是，苹果公司在其市场营销中宣称iPhone具有防水功能，但随后拒绝了遭受水损坏的手机的保修服务。 
此外，鉴于强调广告宣传的耐水性，免责声明“该保证不涵盖由液体引起的损坏”，由于不明确提及哪种类型的保证（常规保证或法律保证），也被认为可能欺骗消费者。它是否被认为能够充分结合防水要求的条件和限制。
 反托拉斯还认为，考虑到苹果在售后阶段拒绝为那些iPhone机型被水或其他液体损坏而兑现保修的做法是适当的，因此剥夺了消费者应享有的保修或保修中的权利。消费者代码。
 苹果为iPhone配备了内部指示器，该指示器可以显示何时有水进入设备。激活此指示灯后，Apple的政策是拒绝保修或更换保修。这意味着买家在购买电话时会被告知一件事，而在需要售后服务时会被告知另一件事。
 苹果已被罚款1000万欧元，并另外下令通过“消费者保护信息”链接在其意大利网站上发布公告。
 潜在问题为潜在的欧盟其他国家的类似裁决开辟了道路，并且可以想象，既然这个问题已经得到了重视，它就可能导致美国和其他地区的集体诉讼。
 SetteBIT指出，该裁决引用了三个Apple广告。苹果公司倾向于从其YouTube频道中删除较旧的广告，但该网站会存档自己的副本，以提供最初提出的声明的证据。

note_en:
Italian regulators have fined  Apple €10M ($12M) for making misleading and unfair claims about  iPhone water resistance.
 The Italian  antitrust authority found that Apple was guilty of two things, one of them more serious than the other …
  The fine was imposed by L’Autorità Garante della Concorrenza e del Mercato ( AGCM), which translates literally as the guarantee authority for competition and the market. This is the competition watchdog responsible for ensuring that companies treat both consumers and competitors fairly.
 First, it says, Apple made water resistance claims without making it clear to consumers that these were true only in ideal laboratory conditions, and phones had not passed the same tests in real-life conditions.
 The first concerns the marketing of a number of different iPhone models – iPhone 8, iPhone 8 Plus, iPhone XR, iPhone XS, iPhone XS Max, iPhone 11, iPhone 11pro and iPhone 11 pro Max – in which it was claimed that each of the advertised products was water resistant to a maximum depth varying between 4 meters and 1 meter depending on the model. for up to 30 minutes.
 According to the Authority, however, the messages did not clarify that these claims were true only in the presence of specific conditions, for example during specific and controlled laboratory tests with the use of static and pure water, and not in normal use of the devices by consumers.
 Second, and more seriously, Apple made iPhone water resistance claims in its marketing, but then refused warranty service on phones which suffered water damage.
 Furthermore, the disclaimer “The guarantee does not cover damage caused by liquids”, given the emphatic advertising boast of water resistance, was considered likely to deceive consumers by not clarifying which type of guarantee it referred to (conventional guarantee or legal guarantee), nor was it deemed capable of adequately contextualizing the conditions and limitations of the claims of water resistance.
 The Antitrust also considered it appropriate to take into account Apple’s refusal, in the post-sales phase, to honor warranties when those iPhone models were damaged by water or other liquids, thus depriving consumers of the rights they should expect from the guarantee or in the Consumer Code.
 Apple fits iPhones with internal indicators which show when there has been water ingress into the device. It is Apple policy to deny warranty repair or replacement when this indicator is activated. This means buyers are told one thing when they buy the phone, but something else when they need after-sales service.
 Apple has been fined ten million Euros, and additionally ordered to publish a notice on its Italian website through a ‘Consumer protection information’ link.
 The potentially opens the way for similar rulings in other European Union countries, and could conceivably lead to class action lawsuits in the US and elsewhere, now that the issue has been highlighted.
 SetteBIT notes that the ruling references three Apple ads. Apple tends to delete older ads from its YouTube channel, but the site archives its own copies, providing proof of the claims originally made.

该法案于周二由州众议院和参议院通过，这是在高级立法者宣布达成协议以结束数月僵局之后的第二天。
 警察改革法案还禁止使用扼流圈和橡皮子弹，并限制使用催泪弹等化学剂，还允许警察干预以防止使用过度和不合理的武力。但是该法案并未消除对警察的资格豁免，这是一项有争议的措施，在受到警察团体的反对之后，该措施使在职警察免受因不当行为而采取的法律行动。
  立法者在乔治·弗洛伊德（George Floyd）被谋杀后将法案送交州立法机构。乔治·弗洛伊德（George Floyd）因被指控谋杀而被一名明尼阿波利斯白人警察杀害。
 批评者多年来一直抱怨面部识别技术存在缺陷，偏见，并且错误地错误地识别了人和颜色社区。但是该法案授予警察例外，可以凭手令针对该州的驾照数据库进行面部识别搜索。为了准予该例外，州政府将必须公布有关官员搜索次数的年度透明度数据。
 马萨诸塞州参议院以28票对12票通过，众议院以92票67票通过。该法案现在将寄给马萨诸塞州州长查理·贝克（Charlie Baker）签名。
 在联邦政府缺乏隐私立法的情况下，限制使用面部识别的法律正在州和市一级出现。该法律的拼凑性质意味着州和城市法律有试验的空间，为将来的法律创建了一系列可在其他地方复制的蓝图。
 俄勒冈州波特兰市今年9月通过了一项广泛的面部识别技术禁令。该禁令是美国最激进的禁令之一，它禁止市政局使用该技术，但还将禁止私人公司在公共场所部署面部识别系统。在那个城市，抗议者与激进的执法部门之间长达数月的冲突，增加了波特兰禁令的赌注。 
奥克兰，旧金山和波士顿早先的禁令着重禁止其市政府使用该技术，但像马萨诸塞州一样，它也一直在限制私有公司的使用。旧金山的禁令于去年5月通过，这使国际技术中心成为首个禁止城市机构和警察部门使用面部识别的主要城市。
 在美国各城市采取行动限制生物识别监控的蔓延的同时，这些系统也在联邦一级传播。 8月，移民与海关执法局（ICE）签署了一项访问由Clearview AI创建的面部识别数据库的合同，该公司是一家备受争议的公司，该公司从在线资源（包括社交媒体网站）中抓取面部图像。
 尽管大多数反对面部识别的行动只涉及地方问题，但至少一项州法律已被证明足以在全国范围内引起轰动。在伊利诺伊州，《生物识别信息隐私法》（BIPA）诱使包括亚马逊，微软和Alphabet在内的主要科技公司未经允许就在伊利诺伊州居民上训练面部识别系统。
note_en:
The  bill was passed by both the state’s House and Senate on Tuesday, a day after senior lawmakers announced an agreement that ended months of deadlock.
 The police reform bill also bans the use of chokeholds and rubber bullets, and limits the use of chemical agents like tear gas, and also allows police officers to intervene to prevent the use of excessive and unreasonable force. But the bill does not remove qualified immunity for police, a controversial measure that shields serving police from legal action for misconduct, following objections from police groups.
  Lawmakers brought the bill to the state legislature in the wake of  the killing of George Floyd, an unarmed Black man who was killed by a white Minneapolis police officer, since charged with his murder.
 Critics have for years complained that facial recognition technology is flawed, biased, and  disproportionately misidentifies people and communities of color. But the bill grants police an exception to run facial recognition searches against the state’s driver’s license database with a warrant. In granting that exception, the state will have to publish annual transparency figures on the number of searches made by officers.
 The Massachusetts Senate voted 28-12 to pass, and the House voted 92-67. The bill will now be sent to Massachusetts governor Charlie Baker for his signature.
 In the absence of privacy legislation from the federal government, laws curtailing the use of facial recognition are popping up on a state and city level. The patchwork nature of that legislation means that state and city laws have room to experiment, creating an array of blueprints for future laws that can be replicated elsewhere.
 Portland, Oregon passed a  broad ban on facial recognition tech this September. The ban, one of the most aggressive in the nation, blocks city bureaus from using the technology but will also prohibit private companies from deploying facial recognition systems in public spaces. Months of clashes between protesters and aggressive law enforcement in that city raised the stakes on Portland’s ban.
 Earlier bans in Oakland, San Francisco, and Boston focused on forbidding their city governments from using the technology but, like Massachusetts, stopped short of limiting its use by private companies. San Francisco’s ban  passed in May of last year, making the international tech hub the first major city to ban the use of facial recognition by city agencies and police departments.
 At the same time that cities across the U.S. are acting to limit the creep of biometric surveillance, those same systems are spreading at the federal level. In August, Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) signed a contract for access to a facial recognition database  created by Clearview AI, a deeply controversial company that scrapes facial images from online sources, including social media sites.
 While most activism against facial recognition only pertains to local issues,  at least one state law has proven powerful enough to make waves on a national scale. In Illinois, the Biometric Information Privacy Act (BIPA) has ensnared major tech companies including Amazon, Microsoft and Alphabet for training facial recognition systems on Illinois residents without permission.
title: 马萨诸塞州立法者投票通过全州警察的面部识别禁令
title_en: Massachusetts Lawmakers Vote To Pass a Statewide Police Ban On Facial Recognition

 ＆＃34;如果幸运的话，也许是四年了，＆＃34;马斯克在德国柏林的颁奖晚会网上直播中说。 ＆＃34;我们希望在两年内将一辆未乘用的车辆寄到那里。
  雄心勃勃的2026年目标与马斯克在2016年9月国际宇航大会上概述的目标相吻合，当时马斯克说，“如果一切进展顺利，”在十年内将人们降落在火星上可能会有点。
 我不想说这是什么时候发生–风险很大，＆＃34;马斯克在2016年说过。 
实现他的火星目标的关键是SpaceX研发的Starship火箭：该公司正在制造的不锈钢运载工具，其目标是一次可运载100人。
 与SpaceX的Falcon 9和Falcon Heavy火箭舰队可部分重用不同，Musk的目的是使Starship完全可重用-设想一种更类似于商用飞机的火箭，其间的周转时间短唯一主要成本是燃油的航班。
  虽然德克萨斯州Boca Chica的星际飞船发展迅速，但发展速度却不及马斯克在2019年9月推出第一枚原型火箭时所希望的那样快。当时，他估计星际飞船可能在2020年3月到达轨道，但现在，SpaceX希望在2021年之前不会进行首次轨道飞行测试。
  此外，尽管马斯克曾说过星舰今年可能会飞人，但他后来承认火箭仍然有许多里程碑，包括“数百次任务”。在那之前发生。
 自公司5月份在其首次载人飞行任务中发射了两名NASA宇航员以来，马斯克就将SpaceX的重点放在了Starship的开发上。 6月，马斯克在CNBC获得的公司范围内的电子邮件中写道，“星际飞船”计划必须迅速并迅速地加快进度。
 从那时起，SpaceX进行了两次短途的，500英尺高的星舰原型飞行测试。该公司目前正在准备进行到50,000英尺高空的首次飞行测试，并有望在未来几天内进行尝试。

新的美国国会法案将要求稳定币发行人在发行任何稳定币之前获得银行宪章并获得监管部门的批准。
 美国代表拉希达·特莱布（D-密歇根州），耶苏斯·“丘伊”·加西亚（D-伊利诺伊州）和史蒂芬·林奇（D·马萨诸塞州）于周三介绍了《稳定币网络共享和银行执照执行法》（STABLE），并在新闻界撰文发布，它将专注于监管稳定币，以Facebook领导的Libra项目（自更名为Diem）命名为稳定币为例。
 新闻稿称：“数字货币的价值与美元等传统货币永久钉住或稳定在一起，在监管方面面临着新的挑战，同时也代表着越来越多的市场，流动性和信贷风险。”
 通过注册，您将收到有关CoinDesk产品的电子邮件，并且您同意我们的条款＆amp;条件和隐私政策。
 长达18页的法案将特别要求稳定币发行人获得银行宪章；需要获得美联储，联邦存款保险公司和发行人的特定州或联邦银行监管机构的批准；要求这些实体对任何系统性风险进行持续分析；并要求发行人购买FDIC保险或保留准备金，以便轻松转换回美元。
  该法案说，这将适用于与其他本国或本州货币挂钩的稳定币。
  众议员特莱布（Tlaib）的经济顾问Chastity Murphy告诉CoinDesk，州和联邦银行宪章都将满足该法案的要求。 
俄勒冈州威拉米特大学法学院助理教授罗汉·格雷（Rohan Grey）告诉CoinDesk，该法案实际上定义了就数字资产而言的存款。在他看来，稳定币实际上是一种互联网本地存款形式。
 他说：“任何想要发行诸如货币或存款之类的走路和说话的实体，都应像托管机构一样受到监管。”格雷是该法案的顾问。
 新闻稿还提到了赞助商和共同赞助商此前寄给货币代理代理局长布莱恩·布鲁克斯（Brian Brooks）的一封信，该信质疑监管机构对数字资产领域的关注。特别是，立法者在为稳定币发行人和其他加密平台提供托管服务的银行中发出了OCC解释性信。
   众议员特莱布在推特和新闻稿中说，该法案旨在保护个人。
 她在一条推文中写道：“防止加密货币提供商重复针对有色传统大银行的中低收入居民的犯罪至关重要。”
 目前，美国有多家没有银行宪章的稳定币发行人，包括CENTER财团（由Circle和Coinbase组成），Gemini和Paxos。诸如base.cash之类的算法稳定币或诸如DAI之类的加密抵押代币似乎也属于该法案之列。
  “符合法定定义的任何稳定币都是合格的，因为重点在于代币的承诺（即义务），而不是它声称如何能够履行义务（即抵押物）。完全保留，部分保留，算法确定的篮子–这些都是资产方面的变化。 “存款”部分着重于责任方承诺，”墨菲说。 
Circle首席执行官杰里米·阿莱尔（Jeremy Allaire）表示，该法案将限制行业创新，“将意味着倒退一大步”。
 “非银行金融科技公司带给了资金不足的小企业的巨大创新，并且迫使加密货币，金融科技和区块链公司承担美联储和FDIC监管的巨大监管负担，这与目标不符。他在发给CoinDesk的一份声明中说：“在稳定和稳定的支付方式中，支持创新以公平，包容的方式进行支付。”
 他说，更有效的监管稳定币的方法可能来自于新章程或其他类型的监管。
  区块链协会执行董事克里斯汀·史密斯（Kristin Smith）在一份声明中反对该法案，并说：“虽然我们与众议员办公室就此问题进行了持续和建设性的讨论，但我们不同意该立法的观点，并反对该法案。这将加强最强大的金融机构的地位，同时又会忽视去中心化网络的两个核心承诺：将更多的权力掌握在个人消费者手中，并促进支付和其他金融服务领域的创新的机会。”
 史密斯（Smith）说，OCC关于稳定币的指南是如何在美国监管这些代币的“一个进步的例子”。
  本届国会会议将在短短几周内结束，但墨菲表示该法案将于明年再次提出。
 他说：“通常在当下对技术变革或创新会做出反应，说出'停止，不要这样做'，而这项法案试图做的就是保持这种前瞻性。” 
披露CoinDesk是区块链新闻的领导者，是一家致力于追求最高新闻标准并遵守一套严格的编辑政策的媒体机构。 CoinDesk是Digital Currency Group的独立运营子公司，该公司投资于加密货币和区块链初创公司。
note_en:
A new U.S. congressional bill would require stablecoin issuers to secure bank charters and secure regulatory approval prior to circulating any stablecoins.
 U.S. Representatives Rashida Tlaib (D-Mich.), Jesús “Chuy” García (D-Ill.) and Stephen Lynch (D-Mass.) introduced the  Stablecoin Tethering and Bank Licensing Enforcement (STABLE) Act on Wednesday, writing in a press release that it would focus on regulating stablecoins, naming the stablecoin of the Facebook-led  Libra project ( since renamed Diem) as one example.
 “Digital currencies, whose value is permanently pegged to or stabilized against a conventional currency like the dollar, pose new regulatory challenges while also represent a growing source of the market, liquidity, and credit risk,” the press release said.
 By signing up, you will receive emails about CoinDesk products and you agree to our  terms & conditions and  privacy policy.
 The 18-page bill would specifically require stablecoin issuers to obtain a banking charter; require approval from the Federal Reserve, Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation and the issuer’s specific state or federal bank regulator; require those same entities to conduct an ongoing analysis of any systemic risk; and require issuers to have FDIC insurance or maintain reserves for easy conversion back into U.S. dollars.
  This would apply to stablecoins pegged to other national or state currencies, the bill said.
  Chastity Murphy, an economic adviser to Rep. Tlaib, told CoinDesk that both state and federal bank charters would satisfy the bill’s requirements.
 Rohan Grey, an assistant professor at the Willamette University College of Law in Oregon, told CoinDesk the bill is really defining what a deposit is as far as digital assets are concerned. In his view, stablecoins are effectively an internet-native form of a deposit.
 “Any entity that wants to issue something that walks and talks like money or like a deposit should be regulated like a depository institution,” he said. Grey is an adviser for the bill.
 The press release also  mentioned a letter that the sponsors and cosponsors had previously sent to Acting Comptroller of the Currency Brian Brooks, that questioned the regulator’s focus on the digital asset space. Specifically, the lawmakers took issue with OCC interpretive letters on banks providing custody services to stablecoin issuers and other crypto platforms.
   The bill is designed to protect individuals, Rep. Tlaib said on Twitter and in the press release.
 In a tweet she wrote, “Preventing cryptocurrency providers from repeating the crimes against low- and moderate-income residents of color traditional big banks have is critically important.”
 A number of stablecoin issuers currently operate in the U.S. without banking charters, including the CENTRE consortium (which is composed of Circle and Coinbase), Gemini and Paxos. Algorithmic stablecoins like basis.cash or crypto collateral tokens like  DAI would also appear to fall under this bill.
  “Any stablecoin that meets the statutory definition is eligible, because the focus is on what the coin promises (i.e., the obligation), not how it claims to be able to enforce the obligation (i.e., the collateral backing). Full-reserve, partial-reserve, algorithmically deteremined basket – these are all asset-side variations. The ‘deposit’ component focuses on the liability side promise,” Murphy said.
 Circle CEO Jeremy Allaire said the bill “would represent a huge step backwards” by limiting industry innovation.
 “An enormous amount of the innovation brought to the underbanked and small businesses has been driven by non-bank fintech companies, and forcing crypto, fintech and blockchain companies into the enormous regulatory burdens of Federal Reserve and FDIC regulation and supervision is inconsistent with the goals of supporting innovation in the fair and inclusive delivery of payments that comes from stablecoins,” he said in a statement emailed to CoinDesk.
 More effective ways to regulate stablecoins could come from new charters or other types of supervision, he said.
  In a statement, Blockchain Association Executive Director Kristin Smith opposed the bill, saying, “While we have had sustained and constructive discussions with Rep. Tlaib’s office on this issue, we disagree with the perspective of this legislation and oppose this bill. It would strengthen the position of the most powerful financial institutions, while overlooking two core promises of decentralized networks: the chance to put more power in the hands of individual consumers and to catalyze innovation across payments and other financial services.”
 Smith said the OCC’s guidance around stablecoins was a “progressive example” of how these tokens could be regulated in the U.S.
  The current congressional session will end in just a few weeks but Murphy said the bill will be introduced again next year.
 “Often there’s a tendency to react in the moment to technological changes or innovation by saying, ‘stop, don’t do that’ and what this bill is trying to do is preserve this in a forward-looking way,” he said.

CoinDesk is an independent operating subsidiary of  Digital Currency Group, which invests in  cryptocurrencies and blockchain  startups.

 现在，Facebook表示将开始删除有关这些疫苗的虚假声明，这些声明已被公共卫生专家揭穿。
 此举是在英国成为第一个批准一种主要冠状病毒疫苗的国家之后进行的。

 Facebook的政策是删除有关Covid-19的虚假声明，因为它声称可能导致迫在眉睫的身体伤害，例如宣传虚假疗法的帖子或将病毒与5G相关的虚假阴谋论。由于这项政策，该公司在3月至10月之间删除了1200万个帖子。

 周一，Facebook首席执行官马克·扎克伯格（Mark Zuckerberg）表示，这家社交媒体巨头将向用户展示＆＃34;有关疫苗的权威信息，＆＃34;无需详细说明它计划如何分发此信息。

  ＆＃34;自从早期以来，关于COVID-19疫苗的事实将继续发展，因此，我们将根据公共卫生当局的指导，在他们了解更多信息的基础上，定期更新我们删除的声明，＆＃34; Facebook添加了。

 对于社交媒体平台来说，解决有关Covid疫苗的错误信息将变得困难，因为全球各国政府都希望开始为人们提供针对该疾病的免疫接种。 
谷歌的YouTube视频共享服务和中国人拥有的社交媒体应用TikTok表示，他们将删除有关此类疫苗的虚假声明，而推特则表示，在获得医学批准的疫苗之前，它正在努力制定计划。

Facebook's policy has been to remove false claims about Covid-19 that it says could lead to "imminent physical harm."
 Now, Facebook said it will "start removing false claims about these vaccines that have been debunked by public health experts."
 The move comes after the U.K. became the first country to approve one of the leading coronavirus vaccines.
 Facebook said Thursday it will start removing misinformation about coronavirus vaccines as countries around the world prepare to start rolling them out.
 Facebook's policy has been to remove false claims about Covid-19 that it says could lead to "imminent physical harm," such as posts promoting  false cures or  bogus conspiracy theories linking the virus to 5G. The company removed 12 million posts as a result of this policy between March and October.
  But it hadn't yet taken a firm stance on vaccines, other than to  ban ads that promote anti-vaccination conspiracy theories. The company has only removed posts containing misinformation about vaccines in Pakistan and Samoa in the past, and those examples weren't related to Covid.
 On Monday, Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg said the social media giant would show its users " authoritative information about the vaccines," without elaborating on how it planned to distribute this information.
 Now, as major pharmaceutical companies like  Pfizer and  AstraZeneca say they've developed  effective Covid vaccines and after the U.K. became the  first country to approve one of the frontrunners, Facebook has outlined a stance to delete false claims about them.
 The company  said it will "start removing false claims about these vaccines that have been debunked by public health experts on Facebook and Instagram."
  "This could include false claims about the safety, efficacy, ingredients or side effects of the vaccines," the company said in a blog post. "For example, we will remove false claims that COVID-19 vaccines contain microchips, or anything else that isn't on the official vaccine ingredient list."
  "Since it's early and facts about COVID-19 vaccines will continue to evolve, we will regularly update the claims we remove based on guidance from public health authorities as they learn more," Facebook added.
 The move comes after a series of measures taken by Facebook to remove problematic content that it had previously been hesitant to take down, such as Holocaust denial and the QAnon conspiracy theory.
 Facebook came under  intense pressure to tackle toxic content earlier in the year, with major brands pausing their advertising on the platform. It also faced a major challenge in avoiding the manipulation of its platform during the U.S. presidential election last month.
 Tackling misinformation about Covid vaccines will prove difficult for social media platforms as governments globally look to start immunizing people against the disease.
 Google's YouTube video-sharing service and Chinese-owned social media app TikTok  have said they will remove false claims about such vaccines, while Twitter says it's working through its plans before a medically approved vaccine becomes available.

title_en: Facebook says it will start removing false claims about coronavirus vaccines that have been debunked by public health experts

金融数据提供商标准普尔全球公司（S＆amp; P Global Inc.）的子公司标普道琼斯指数（S＆amp; P Dow Jones Indices）周四表示，它将在2021年推出加密货币指数，使其成为最新进入这一新兴资产类别的大型金融公司。
  两家公司表示，标普DJI品牌产品将使用来自纽约虚拟货币公司Lukka的数据，交易超过550种顶级交易硬币。
 标普和卢卡在联合声明中表示，标准普尔的客户将能够与指数提供商合作，以创建针对加密货币的定制索引和其他基准工具。
 他们说，标准普尔和卢卡证券公司希望更可靠的定价数据将使投资者更容易获得新的资产类别，并减少极度动荡和投机市场的某些风险。
  标普道琼斯指数全球创新与战略负责人彼得·罗夫曼说：“随着数字资产（例如加密货币）成为一种快速崛起的资产类别，现在正是建立独立，可靠和用户友好基准的合适时机。”
 全球最知名的索引提供商之一的举动可能有助于加密货币成为更为主流的投资。
 随之而来的是，由于投资者的需求增加，比特币兑美元继续飙升至历史新高，投资者称虚拟货币是对冲通胀和避险资产的避险工具。 
比特币周四最新交易价格为19,300美元，今年飙升了约170％。  加密货币已经存在了十多年，但是在过去几年中已经开始吸引大型金融公司的更多兴趣。  包括富达投资（Fidelity Investments）和日本野村控股公司（Nomura Holdings Inc.）在内的大公司已经开始为机构投资者保护比特币和其他加密货币，而大型交易所已经开始提供比特币衍生品。  更加主流的市场基础设施的出现使资产类别更容易为机构投资者所用，诸如Paul Tudor Jones和Stanley Druckenmiller等对冲基金经理表示，他们将比特币纳入其广泛的投资策略中。
note_en:
LONDON (Reuters) - S&P Dow Jones Indices, a division of financial data provider S&P Global Inc, said on Thursday that it will launch cryptocurrency indices in 2021, making it the latest major finance company to enter the nascent asset class.
  The S&P DJI-branded products will use data from New York-based virtual currency company Lukka on more than 550 of the top traded coins, the companies said.
 S&P’s clients will be able to work with the index provider to create customized indices and other benchmarking tools on cryptocurrencies, S&P and Lukka said in a joint statement.
 S&P and Lukka hope more reliable pricing data will make it easier for investors to access the new asset class, and reduce some of the risks of the very volatile and speculative market, they said.
  “With digital assets such as cryptocurrencies becoming a rapidly emerging asset class, the time is right for independent, reliable and user-friendly benchmarks,” said Peter Roffman, global head of innovation and strategy at S&P Dow Jones Indices.
 The move by one of the world’s most well-known index providers could help cryptocurrencies become more mainstream investments.
 It comes as bitcoin continues to soar to record highs against the dollar, boosted by increased demand from investors who say the virtual currency is a hedge against inflation and a safe-haven asset.
  Bitcoin was trading at $19,300 in latest trading on Thursday, having soared around 170% this year.
 Cryptocurrencies have been around for more than a decade, but have started attracting more interest from large financial companies over the last few years.
 Large firms including Fidelity Investments and Japan’s Nomura Holdings Inc have starting safeguarding bitcoins and other cryptocurrencies for institutional investors, while major exchanges have started offering bitcoin derivatives.
 The emergence of more mainstream market infrastructure has made the asset class more accessible for institutional investors, with hedge fund managers such as Paul Tudor Jones and Stanley Druckenmiller saying they include bitcoin in their broad investment strategies.
title: 标普道琼斯指数（S＆P Dow Jones Indices）表示，它将使用加密公司Lukka的数据对550多种交易量最高的硬币使用2021年推出加密货币指数
title_en: S&P Dow Jones Indices says it will launch cryptocurrency indices in 2021, using data from crypto company Lukka on more than 550 of the top traded coins

正如微软公司Azure数据公司副总裁Rohan Kumar告诉我的那样，这已经成为企业的主要痛点。尽管他们可能对使用诸如预测分析之类的数据密集型技术感到非常兴奋，但这些公司的数据和注重隐私的高管非常担心，以确保数据的使用方式合规或公司获得了正确的使用权。例如，使用其客户数据的权限。
 此外，公司还希望确保他们可以信任自己的数据，并知道谁有权访问数据以及谁进行了更改。
  “ [Purview]是一个统一的数据治理平台，可自动执行数据发现，数据分类，数据映射，沿袭跟踪-旨在使我们的客户很好地了解开始存在的数据产业的广度并确保所有符合要求的法规（例如GDPR，CCPA等）都在整个数据仓库中进行管理，以确保您不违反任何法规的方式进行。” 。
 Purview的核心是目录，该目录可以从常见的可疑对象（例如Azure的各种数据和存储服务）中提取数据，也可以从第三方数据存储中提取数据，包括Amazon的S3存储服务和本地SQL Server。随着时间的推移，该公司将增加对更多数据源的支持。
 Kumar将这一过程描述为“多学期的投资”，因此该公司今天推出的功能仅占总体路线图的一小部分。在今天的第一个版本中，重点是映射公司的数据仓库。
  “接下来（在路线图上）将有更多的治理政策，”库马尔说。 “想象一下，如果您要设置诸如'如果我的任何数据存储中都存在任何PII数据，那么只有这一组用户才可以访问它。'今天，设置这样的操作非常复杂，很可能您会得到这是错误的。这就像在Purview中设置政策一样简单。”
 除了启动Purview之外，Azure团队还于今天将Microsoft的下一代数据仓库和分析服务Azure Synapse投入了可用性。 Synapse背后的想法是为企业及其工程师和数据科学家提供一个单一平台，该平台将数据集成，仓储和大数据分析结合在一起。 
“通过Synapse，我们拥有一款产品，它为数据工程师提供了完全的无代码体验，例如，可以构建这些[data]管道并与正在构建机器学习模型的数据科学家无缝地协作，或者 为诸如Power BI之类的报告构建报告的业务分析师。”  微软（FedEx），沃尔格林（Walgreens），Myntra和宝洁（P＆amp; G）是该服务的大批客户，库马尔将其描述为目前增长最快的Azure服务之一。  FedEx Services战略计划高级副总裁Sriram Krishnasamy说：“我们从持续分析中获得的见解有助于我们优化网络。” “因此，随着FedEx在全球范围内运送重要的高价值货物时，我们通常可以预测该交付是否会因天气或交通而中断，并通过从其他位置路由该交付来补救这种中断。”
note_en:
As Rohan Kumar,  Microsoft’s   corporate VP for Azure Data, told me, this has become a major pain point for enterprises. While they may be very excited about getting started with data-heavy technologies like predictive analytics, those companies’ data and privacy-focused executives are very concerned to make sure that the way the data is used is compliant or that the company has received the right permissions to use its customers’ data, for example.
 In addition, companies also want to make sure that they can trust their data and know who has access to it and who made changes to it.
  “[Purview] is a unified data governance platform which automates the discovery of data, cataloging of data, mapping of data, lineage tracking — with the intention of giving our customers a very good understanding of the breadth of the data estate that exists to begin with, and also to ensure that all these regulations that are there for compliance, like GDPR, CCPA, etc, are managed across an entire data estate in ways which enable you to make sure that they don’t violate any regulation,” Kumar explained.
 At the core of Purview is its catalog that can pull in data from the usual suspects, like Azure’s various data and storage services, but also third-party data stores, including Amazon’s S3 storage service and on-premises SQL Server. Over time, the company will add support for more data sources.
 Kumar described this process as a “multi-semester investment,” so the capabilities the company is rolling out today are only a small part of what’s on the overall road map already. With this first release today, the focus is on mapping a company’s data estate.
  “Next [on the road map] is more of the governance policies,” Kumar said. “Imagine if you want to set things like ‘if there’s any PII data across any of my data stores, only this group of users has access to it.’ Today, setting up something like that is extremely complex and most likely you’ll get it wrong. That’ll be as simple as setting a policy inside of Purview.”
 In addition to launching Purview, the Azure team also today launched into general availability Azure Synapse, Microsoft’s next-generation data warehousing and analytics service. The idea behind Synapse is to give enterprises — and their engineers and data scientists — a single platform that brings together data integration, warehousing and big data analytics.
  “With Synapse, we have this one product that gives a completely no-code experience for data engineers, as an example, to build out these [data] pipelines and collaborate very seamlessly with the data scientists who are building out machine learning models, or the business analysts who build out reports for things like Power BI.”
 Among Microsoft’s marquee customers for the service, which Kumar described as one of the fastest-growing Azure services right now, are FedEx, Walgreens, Myntra and P&G.
 “The insights we gain from continuous analysis help us optimize our network,” said Sriram Krishnasamy, senior vice president, strategic programs at FedEx Services. “So as FedEx moves critical high-value shipments across the globe, we can often predict whether that delivery will be disrupted by weather or traffic and remediate that disruption by routing the delivery from another location.”
title: 微软推出了Azure Purview，这是一个统一的数据治理服务，可以在公共预览中自动进行数据的发现，分类，映射和跟踪。
title_en: Microsoft launches Azure Purview, a unified data governance service that automates the discovery, cataloging, mapping, and tracking of data, in public preview
